Language.name: English
Language.String:
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Home City Shipments are earned slightly faster. Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Pikeman (Tericos), Rodelero (Espadachins), Lancer (Garrochistas) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
War Dog, Missionary, Rodelero, Lancer, Gatling Gun and Gaucho
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Lombard 

The succesor to the Viceroyalty of the Rio de la Plata, following the end of its long civil war in 1861 Argentina enjoyed a relative era of peace for the remainder of the 19th Century, and experienced a large influx of European Immigrants, particularly Italians.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Can train Privateers with increased build limit, and which are allowed to train several units.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Barbary Warrior, Corsair Marksman, Tribal Horseman, Bedouin Horse Archers, Muhbir and Great Bombard

From the late Middle Ages on, Piracy was rampant in the Western Medditerranian based in a number of North African states. In 1805 US Marines led sucessful action against Tripoli, which initiated the decline of the Barbary States in the 19th Century.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Receives a free Covered Wagon with every Age and starts with 7 Citizens, which cost 70 coin. Town Centers automatically spawn Voluntarios Da Patria, which are weaker but faster Revolutionaries.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Musketeer (Legionarios), Dragoon (Legion Dragoons), Crossbowman (Order Besteiros), Pikeman (Ordinance Pikemen) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Volunterio Da Patria and Gatling Gun

Portugal begain settling what is now Brazil in the 16th Century. In 1822, Brazil declared independence from Portugal and established the Empire of Brazil, which lasted until 1889.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Cows gather more from Haciendas and you can have 3 times as many cows. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Industrial Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Chinaco (Charro), Salteador (Vigilante) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Padre, Insurgente, Salteador, Soldado, Chinaco, Californio
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

 On June 14th 1846, American immigrants declared indipendence from Mexico in San Fransisco. 25 days later, the rebels were absorbed into the California Battalion of the American Army.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Manor spawns a Metis Pathfinder when built. Villagers gain extra ranged attack with each Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Musketeer (Redcoats), Hussar (King's Life Guard) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Ranger and Congreve Rocket

 British Colonization of Newfoundland began in the 16th Century. With victory in the Seven Years War, Britain took control of French Canadian holdings. Canada remained loyal to the British during the American Indipendence War and was an important front in the War of 1812. In 1867 Britain granted greater autonomy to the Canadian Dominion.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Artillery can be trained early and Forts can be built in Commerce Age. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Commerce Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Chinaco (Charro), Salteador (Vigilante) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Padre, Insurgente, Salteador, Soldado, Chinaco
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

The Federal Republic of Central America first declared its indipendence from Spain in 1821 and then from the Mexican Empire in 1823. After their second civil war ended in 1840, the Federal Republic dissolved and the constituent States gained sovereignty.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Home City Shipments are earned slightly faster. Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Pikeman (Tericos), Rodelero (Espadachins), Lancer (Garrochistas), Hussar (Hussar of Death) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
War Dog, Missionary, Rodelero, Lancer, Gatling Gun

 Following the conquest of the Incas, Spanish colonization of Chile began in earnest, where they faced stiff resistance from the Mapuche and other native tribes. In 1810, after Napoleon's deposition of the Spanish monarchy, Chile established an autonomous loyalist government, but morphed into full indipendece by 1818. The century that followed included much conflict with its neighbors, as well as internal wars.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Home City Shipments are earned slightly faster. Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Musketeers are replaced with Indipendece Guards, which have a bonus against Cavalry at range, and with each Age gain additional attack and hitpoints but lose speed.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Pikeman (Tericos), Rodelero (Espadachins), Lancer (Garrochistas)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
War Dog, Missionary, Rodelero, Lancer, Gatling Gun

Spanish Conquistadors landed in Colombia early in the 16th Century in search of gold. Named "New Granada" it was initially incorporated into the Viceroyalty of Peru, before having its own Viceroyalty of New Granada established. Quickly it became known for its scientific minds in biology and astronomy. Colombia declared indipendence in 1810 but its Indipendence war would not conclude until 1822.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Khevite Fusiliers replace Revolutionaries, which can change their stats with their stance. Can train Mamelukes and Stradiot from the Stable, Forts, and Galleons, and receive 1 free Mameluke with each Shipment starting in Fortress Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Deli (Rumeliot) and Humbaraci (Ulufeli)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Azap, Janissary, Imam, Bashibozuk, Muhbir, Sipahi, Deli, Abus Gunner, Humbaraci, Great Bombard and Galley

In 1517 the Ottoman Empire subjegated Mamluke Egypt. After Napoleon's 1798 Egyptian campaign, an internal power struggle resulted in Muhammad Ali Pasha outsing the Mamlukes from power, and carved out defacto independence. The Ottomans declared Egypt a Khedivate, or autonomous vassal state, in 1867, and it was soon after that the Suez Canal was completed in partnership with France.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Can build up to 7 Saloons. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Commerce Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Chinaco (Charro), Salteador (Vigilante) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Padre, Insurgente, Salteador, Soldado, Chinaco, Filibuster
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

In 1853 William Walker led a company of Filibusters to capture La Paz and declared himself president of the new Republic of Baja California. After several months resistance by Mexican locals and armed forces, Walker surrendered himself to the US Navy and was charged with violating the Neutrality Act, but was acquitted by his jury.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Torps gather nearby resources. Advanced improvements are available in the Arsenal. Can train Karelian Jaegers in addition to Settlers, which can build some buildings and gather some resources. Can build up to 6 Taverns. Jaeger can always be trained from Taverns.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Pikeman (Dalkarl), Hakkapelit (Drabant) and Grenadier (Pavlov Grenadiers)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Carolean, Karelian Jaeger, and Hakkapelit
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Blockhouses are a combination of Barracks and Outpost.

Finland was drawn into Sweden's influence startung in the Middle Ages by crusade and Swedish settlement. Finnish soldiers distinguished themselves in the Thirty Years' War with their Hakkapelitta. Russia occupied Finland several times in the 18th Century, but it wasn't until 1809 that Sweden lost control of Finland, at which time it was incorporated into the Russian Empire as the semi-autonomous Grand Duchy of Finland.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Begins with a Native Scout. Trains Metis Voyageurs instead of Settlers. Villagers gain extra ranged attack with each Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Skirmisher (Voltigeurs), Cuirassier (Gendarmes)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Metis Voyageurs and Cuirassier

The settlement of what became New France began with Jacques Cartier's expedition to the Gulf of St. Lawrence in 1534. Eventually the territory stretched from Louisiana to Newfoundland. The main export of the territory was the fur trade, made possible by the relatively good relations between the native peoples and the French settlers. With France's defeat in the Seven Years' War it ceded its North American possesions to Britain and Spain, although French Canadian culture endured centuries on.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Begins with a Native Scout. Town Centers and Taverns slowly spawn Pirates for free. Can train Privateers, which are allowed to train Pirates. Maroons replace Revolutionaries, which are cheaper and have more range and speed, but less hitpoints.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Maroon

The Island of Hispaniola was the site of Cristopher Columbus' first landing in the New World. Spain ceded the western half of the island to France, and the sugar plantations worked by slaves would make it France's most profitable colony. The slaves fought a revolution from 1791 to 1804 and sucessfully expelled the French. The next century was marked by much internal hardship and political instabilty.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Starting in the Commerce Age, receive an Outpost Wagon with every Home City Shipment. Outposts when built spawn a new unit in each Age: a Hajduk, a Grenadier, a Pandour, and a Hussar. Grenzers replace Revolutionaries, which have more range and can pick up treasures, but have less hitpoints. Hungarian Grenadiers replace Grenadiers, which have more range and can be trained from the Barracks and Outposts.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Hussar (Magyar Hussar)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Grenzer, Hungarian Grenadier and Galley

Descendents of the Magyars who fought Otto the Great, Hungary was a powerful state in the late Middle Ages and an important bulwark against Ottoman expansion into Europe, but was subsumed into the Habsburg patrimony in the 18th Century. Despite the nominal faliure of the 1848 revolution, this led the way for greater importance within the Habsburg Empire.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Can train Wokou Junks.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Halberdier (Stadswacht), Ruyter (Carabineer)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Ruyter, Javanese Spearmen, Cetbang Cannon and Fluyt

The Dutch East India Company was formed in 1602 for the purpose of controlling the spice trade in the region now known as Indonesia, but was nationalized in 1799 following its bankruptcy. Dutch hegemony of Indonesia was often tenous and varied from region to region, with a number of revolts across its colonial history culminating in the Aceh War at the end of the 19th Century.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Can train Holcan Javelineers and research Mayan technologies in the Native Embassy, and the build limit of Holcan increase with each Home City Shipment. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Imperial Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Salteador (Vigilante) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Native Villager, Padre, Yucateco Insurgente, Cruzob Avenger, Cruzob Infantry, Chaac Canoe
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

After the conquest of the Aztecs, the Spanish and their native allies turned towards the many Mayan states of the Yucatan. It took nearly 200 years for the Maya to be completely subjegated, and even afterwords Mayan culture persisted. During the chaotic second Yucatan Republic, many Mayan communities rose in revolt. From 1847 until the end of the century, the Maya achieved defacto independence centered around Chan Santa Cruz.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Home City Shipments are earned slightly faster. Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Gains Simon Bolivar upon reaching Fortress Age, who slightly boosts the hitpoints of nearby infantry and cavalry.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Pikeman (Tericos), Rodelero (Espadachins), Lancer (Garrochistas)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
War Dog, Missionary, Rodelero, Lancer, Gatling Gun

The Quechuan Inca forged an empire across the 15th Century in modern Peru, but were in turn conquered by the Spanish with native auxilleries in the 16th Century. The new Viceroyalty of Peru became an important source of mineral wealth for the Spanish Empire, but when the flow of bullion slowed in the 18th Century the reforms meant to address it only resulted in civil unrest, epitomized in the Tupac Rebellion. In the early 19th Century, with Spanish power waning as a consequence of Napoleon's invasion, South America erupted into a patchwork of independence wars, but Peru remained a loyalist stronghold. That is, until forces led by Simon Bolivar and Jose de San Martin, heroes to the Independent Colombia and Argentina respectively, brought their armies to bear against the divided loyalists and by the end of 1824 had soundly defeated Spanish forces.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Trains Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Manor spawns a Citizen when built. Forts spawn 2 Gatling Guns when built. Gains a General upon reaching Fortress Age, who can plant an Inspiring Flag.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Gatling Gun and Congreve Rocket

Starting in the 17th Century from small settlements like those of Jamestown and Plymouth, British of various backgrounds moved to the New World and organized themselves into 13 Colonies stretching the eastern coast of North America. Following the Seven Years War, the British Parliment levied additional taxes upon the colonists to try and recouperate its cost, without the assent of the colonies. These and other grievences convinced most of the American elite that Independence was prefferable, and with the eventual assistance of France and Spain fought and won that right.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Trains Coureurs instead of Settlers and keeps a stronger conventional economy from the opening seconds. Gains an Eclaireur (mounted scout) upon reaching Commerce Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Skirmisher (Voltigeurs), Cuirassier (Gendarmes)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Eclaireur and Cuirassier

Napoleon Bonaparte rose from the chaos of the French Revolutionary Wars to dominate the French state as First Consul and then Emperor. His regime preserved many of the Revolution's military reforms, but redirected France away from radical republican upheaval and toward a centralized imperial war machine built on discipline, artillery, and mass mobilization.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Trains Sans Culottes instead of Settlers, which are stronger in combat but gather more slowly. Gains an Eclaireur (mounted scout) upon reaching Commerce Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Skirmisher (Voltigeurs), Cuirassier (Gendarmes)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Sans Culottes, Eclaireur and Cuirassier

In 1789 with debt and social discontent mounting, Louis XVI convoked the Estates General soliciting advice from his subjects. However, the Third Estate, representing the commoners but helmed by the Mercantile class and New Rich, boldly siezed control and repurposed it into the National Assembly in a bid for sovereignty. Guided by Enlightenment thinkers such as Voltaire and Rousseau, the revolutionaries targeted first the Nobility, then the Clergy, and finally the Monarchy. The new French Republic, fueled by a zealous revolutionary mania, sought to remake the whole of French society, and its Committee enacted a reign of terror upon the citizens of Paris, as well as revolutionary wars of expansion, seeking to spread their ideals by sword and cannon. In 1799, the charismatic general Napoleon Bonaparte siezed control of the fracturous government, first as Consul and then Emperor of the French, and continued his war against all Europe for another 15 years. This quarter century of near constant bloodshed left an indelable mark upon Europe and shaped the course of world events for centuries to come.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Home City Shipments are earned slightly faster. Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Can train Mexican Outlaws from the Saloon, in addition to local Outlaws.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Pikeman (Tericos), Rodelero (Espadachins), Chinaco (Charro)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
War Dog, Missionary, Rodelero, Chinaco, Cuerudo

Spanish explorers had only discovered the east coast of Mexico in 1518, but by 1521 rouge conquistador Hernan Cortez had conquered the Aztec Empire with the help of locals subjegated by the Aztecs. Based in Mexico city, this colony dubbed the Viceroyalty of New Spain slowly expanded from California to Panama over the next two centuries, along the way fighting various native tribes as well as rival European powers. With Napoleon's invasion of Spain in 1808, Mexican elites siezed the moment to argue for home rule. The next 70 years were marked by political instability, civil war, and revolution.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Can research Guard level improvements in Fortress Age but take somewhat longer to research before Industrial Age. May train Mortars, Horse Artillery, and Heavy Cannons an age early. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Fortress Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Chinaco (Charro), Salteador (Vigilante) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Padre, Insurgente, Salteador, Soldado, Chinaco
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

With the settlement of the 1824 Constitution, Mexico was made a Federal Republic. In the 1830's, President Santa Anna centralized the Mexican state under himself, suspended the Mexican Constitution, and abolished the Congress. Resistance to the central government coalesed in northern Mexico along the Rio Grande, and in 1840 the Republic of the Rio Grande was declared. Covertly supported by the Republic of Texas, the Rio Grande army won several victories against Santa Anna's forces but ultimately were reabsorbed into Mexico by 1841.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Starting in the Commerce Age, receive an Outpost Wagon with every Home City Shipment. Outposts when built spawn a Rosior Dragoon, which have more speed and attack but less hitpoints than a normal Dragoon. Dorabants replace Revolutionaries, which have more hitpoints but less range and are more expensive.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Dorabant, Rosior Dragoon and Galley

Following the Mongol yoke, the Romanian principalities of Wallacia, Moldavia, and Transylvania fell under Ottoman hegemony. The three joined the Holy League against the Ottomans in 1594, and were briefly united under Micheal the Brave. Transylvania was incorporated into the Habsburg domain in the 17th century, but not without difficulty. The Ottomans sent Phanariotes to oversee Moldavia and Wallachia but the ensuing instability allowed the Habsburgs and Russians to take more territory. Taking inspiration from the Greek Revolution, revolt arose first in 1821 and again in 1848, but Romania would not be fully independent until 1877.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Villagers and Banks gather faster. Receives a free Trek Wagon with every Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Halberdier (Stadswacht), Ruyter (Carabineer)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Ruyter, Trek Wagon and Fluyt
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Banks to automatically produce coin

Portugese explorer Bartolomeu Dias would be the first to round the Cape of Good Hope in 1487, but it wasn't until the 1650's that Dutch settlers would begin to arive, establishing Cape Town as a supply stop for the Dutch East India Company between Europe and Asia. The British would occupy Cape Town in 1795 and again in 1806 to deny it from the French, who had themselves occupied the Low Countries at this time. With the Congress of Vienna, South Africa was ceded permanently to the British Empire, and British Settlers began arriving in earnest. Seeking to escape the British colonial administration, many descendents from Dutch Settlers, known as Boers, began to migrate by wagon to interior South Africa, now called the Great Trek. The Boers would come into conflict with the Xhosa, Zulu, and by the end of the 19th Century the British themselves.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Minutemen levies can be called repeatedly from Town Centers and Outposts. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Industrial Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Chinaco (Charro), State Militia (Volunteer) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Padre, State Militia, Regular, Sharpshooter, Minuteman, Chinaco, Gatling Gun
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

Spain long considered Texas part of its territory, but only seriously enforced it when the French constructed Fort Saint Louis. With Mexican independence from Spain, Texas became part of Mexico. Many Americans immigrated to Texas at this time, but when Santa Anna sought to centralize the Mexican government under himself, the new Texans thought this a tyrany. They rose in rebellion and declared independence in 1836 as the Texas Republic. The Texans had ongoing conflicts with the Mexicans and Comanche for the next decade, before being annexed by the United States in 1845.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Insurgentes are improved to Yucateco Insurgentes, which gain a Lasso charged attack. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Fortress Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Chinaco (Charro), Salteador (Vigilante) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Padre, Yucateco Insurgente, Salteador, Soldado, Chinaco
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

The Yucatan peninsula's remoteness from Mexico City and challenging geography long made it difficult to control by the Viceroyalty of New Spain. With Mexican Indipendence War beginning in 1810, the local Yucatan elite proposed their own republic. The Yucatan was incorporated into Mexico peacefully in 1824, as the new constitution aligned with their own federal ideals, but with the presidency of Santa Anna the Mexican state became much more centralized. Thus in 1840 the Republic of the Yucatan declared its Indipendence from Mexico, however after 8 years of sporradic conflict they would be reunited.
Chilean Hussar attack and hitpoints greatly increased.
Hungarian Hussar attack and hitpoints greatly increased.
Imperial upgrade for Bedouin Horse Archers.
You receive a free Mameluke with all your shipments, starting with this one.
Notably increases the melee damage of Cavalry Archers and Halberdier. Cavalry Archers inflict area damage in melee.
Observer-only control. Fires the naval observation window early so docks, fishing, and fleet movement can be inspected without waiting for the normal timer.
Observer console: the water window was triggered manually from the Town Center.
Observer-only control. Fires the commander behavior window early so rout, elite support, elite retreat, and formation behavior can be inspected without playing the economy forward.
Observer console: the commander window was triggered manually from the Town Center.
Observer-only control. Grants the repeat-fight rebuild package early so the same engagement can be rerun after the first swing.
Observer console: the repeat-fight window was triggered manually from the Town Center.
Language.string: Forte de Santo Antonio de Gurupá
Forte de São Francisco Xavier de Piratininga
Casa da Torre de Garcia d'Ávila
Forte de Santa Cruz de Itamaracá
Forte de Santo Antônio da Barra
Forte de Santo Antônio Além do Carmo
Fortaleza de Santa Cruz da Barra
Fortaleza de São João da Barra do Rio de Janeiro
Forte de São Paulo da Gambôa
Forte de São João da Bertioga
José de Barros Falcão de Lacerda
San Carlos de la Barra Fortress
Castillo de Santa Rosa de la Eminencia
Fuerte de San Sebastián del Pastelillo
José Inácio de Abreu e Lima
Pierre Gaultier de Varennes, sieur de La Vérendrye
Étienne de Veniard, Sieur de Bourgmont
Louis de la Corne, Chevalier de la Corne
Pierre de Troyes, Chevalier de Troyes
Presidio de San Antonio de Béjar
Language.String: Flat 2v2 test arena for Legendary Leaders AI behavior, including AI rout, elite support, elite retreat, and coordinated team fights.
Open west-vs-east Texas-style test field with three land lanes and two naval lanes for fast AI validation.
Custom validation maps for Legendary Leaders AI skirmish testing.
